steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The nitrogen-treated SiC fiber exhibited a lower minimum creep rate compared to the two polymer-derived fibers. Since minimum creep rate measures how quickly a material deforms under stress, a lower rate indicates superior resistance to deformation.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B states that composites with the nitrogen-treated fiber can withstand stress longer than those with polymer-derived fibers—precisely what the superior creep resistance implies.

The other choices either contradict the finding, add unsupported conditions, or don't follow from the comparison.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
